title: camelina sativa subsp. dentata
intro: Camelina sativa subsp. dentata, a subspecies of the camelina sativa plant, is a flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of polyunsaturated fatty acids, particularly alpha-linolenic acid. The health benefits of camelina sativa subsp. dentata include reducing inflammation, improving heart health, and providing potential protection against certain diseases, such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. Camelina sativa subsp. dentata is commonly prepared as an oil or tincture, which can be used in herbal remedies or supplements to harness its potential health benefits.
